general.title: SSHR - SSH Register CLI
general.description: Manage SSH connections through aliases without manually editing ~/.ssh/config

register.description:
DESCRIPTION
    Register a new SSH connection in the SSHR config file.
register.format:
SUPPORTED INPUTS
    user@host:port
    user@host
    host:port
    host
register.details:
DETAILS
    • Automatically detects user, host and port
    • Port defaults to 22 when omitted
    • Supports both IP addresses and hostnames
register.example:
VALID EXAMPLES
    ✓ sshr -r alice@192.168.1.20:2200
    ✓ sshr -r root@server
    ✓ sshr -r 192.168.1.20
    ✓ sshr -r server:2222
register.seealso:
SEE ALSO
    sshr --help ERR001
    sshr --help ERR014

list.description:
DESCRIPTION
    Display all registered SSH connections.
list.details:
DETAILS
    • -l  shows a compact one-line summary per connection
    • -ll shows detailed information for every field
list.seealso:
SEE ALSO
    sshr --help delete
    sshr --help edit

delete.description:
DESCRIPTION
    Delete an existing SSH connection interactively.
delete.details:
DETAILS
    • Displays the registered connection list
    • Prompts for selection using the connection number
    • Requests confirmation before deletion

edit.description:
DESCRIPTION
    Edit an existing SSH connection.
edit.details:
DETAILS
    • Command currently under development
    • Future versions will support interactive editing

build.description:
DESCRIPTION
    Initialize the SSHR configuration structure.
build.details:
DETAILS
    • Uses XDG_CONFIG_HOME and falls back to ~/.config
    • Creates ~/.config/sshr if needed
    • Copies the default config.ini into the SSHR config folder
    • Reads SSH_DIR from config.ini
    • Creates ~/.ssh and ~/.ssh/config if they do not exist

help.description:
DESCRIPTION
    Show general or detailed help information.
help.details:
DETAILS
    • Without arguments shows general help
    • Accepts command names as help topics
    • Accepts error codes for detailed explanations
help.example:
VALID EXAMPLES
    ✓ sshr -h
    ✓ sshr -h register
    ✓ sshr -h ERR012
